title: Field Lines, Repetition, Combination, Structured Fields, and Limits · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective loss-aware bounded field-section model; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in loss-aware bounded field-section model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon.
Preserve field-line order and repeated values, combining only when that field definition permits it.
Parse Connection options and nominate hop-by-hop fields dynamically instead of relying only on a fixed list.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Field Name Value Grammar
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 3056 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve repeated field lines and reject sections beyond a bounded policy; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Competency probe: Bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Field Resource Disclosure, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Logging every raw field is necessary for parser debugging and has no security cost.
